cite-citadel — example wikis

An LLM-maintained, fully-cited personal wiki in Open Knowledge Format. Below are the demo wikis built from cite-citadel's test corpora — pick one to browse.

These are TEST CORPORA — inputs used to verify cite-citadel's ingest, citation and contradiction-handling behaviour, not reference material. Some deliberately contain planted errors, contradictions, or entirely fictional “facts” (for example the beverages wiki has intentional mistakes, and kelvarra describes a fictional world that is wrong about reality on purpose). Do not treat any wiki here as a source of real-world knowledge. Every page is machine-generated from the raw sources by an LLM agent.

beverages

The everyday coffee & tea showcase: cited facts, merged pages, surfaced contradictions.

Open demo →

kelvarra

A fictional republic whose facts contradict reality, preserved exactly as stated and cited (never “corrected”).

Open demo →

leuchtfeuer

Three years of a fictional project (minutes, German Protokolle, emails) with supersession, reconciliation and deletions.

Open demo →

pemberley

Jane Austen’s Pride and Prejudice folded into a relationship/character wiki.

Open demo →

injection-resistance

Sources that try to hijack the agent; their instructions are quoted, never obeyed.

Open demo →

clockwork

A whole git repository folded in as one digest, then re-ingested after a commit that changes a documented default.

Open demo →

flurfunk

Informal sources — a chat export, a tweet thread, an interview, a forum thread — with attribution kept intact and an in-thread reversal.

Open demo →

gazette

Generated PDFs read in “images” mode: a chart’s number and a scanned notice that live only in the pixels, plus an academic preprint.

Open demo →

kontor

Office documents — OOXML and legacy OLE — with an embedded-chart image delta, dedup-by-basename, and ignore-patterns.

Open demo →
Built with cite-citadel. Each viewer is a self-contained offline HTML page regenerated from its committed showcase wiki on every push.
